_meta.license: Journalism source — patterns paraphrased for design reference. No verbatim text reproduced.
_meta.attribution: California-state disclosure patterns and red-flag categories paraphrased from CalMatters reporting on the AB 252 disclosure framework.
_meta.caveat: California-specific patterns may not map to other states. Verify against the operative state law before drawing reviewer conclusions.

redFlagPatterns.name: Payor associated with collective
redFlagPatterns.summary: Brand payor shares registered-agent overlap with an active NIL collective. Bylaw 22 association test is the gating review.

redFlagPatterns.name: Non-cash value missing
redFlagPatterns.summary: Loaner vehicles, gear allocations, and product seeding rotate through disclosure with inconsistent non-cash-value declarations.

redFlagPatterns.name: Aggregating threshold approach
redFlagPatterns.summary: Multiple smaller deals on the same athlete trip-aggregating thresholds when summed across reporting periods.

redFlagPatterns.name: Category-exclusive overlap
redFlagPatterns.summary: Secondary deal in a category overlapping an exclusive master agreement — requires carve-out memo.

redFlagPatterns.name: Out-of-state payor with no in-state nexus
redFlagPatterns.summary: Payor entity registered out-of-state with no in-state activation nexus — secondary review required.

californiaAB252Note: California AB 252 (2024) requires post-graduation NIL deals to be disclosed within 14 days of execution. Several patterns above derive from CalMatters reporting on early-cycle filings.
